description: This figure presents X-ray elemental distribution maps (EDS mapping) showing the cross-section of a large oxide nodule formed on Fe-7.5Mn-5Al alloy after isothermal oxidation at 800°C for 24 hours in pure oxygen at 200 torr pressure. The image reveals a high degree of enrichment of manganese and aluminum concentrated in the inner Fe3O4 layer and at the gas-oxide interface, corresponding to the thin grey semicontinuous outer oxide layer observed in the accompanying SEM micrograph (Fig. 4). The nodule structure comprises an outer white layer of Fe2O3 and an inner layer of (Fe,Mn)3O4, with the X-ray maps specifically demonstrating that manganese enrichment at the gas-oxide interface characterizes alloys containing more than approximately 5% manganese. This elemental distribution supports the finding that alloys with approximately 7.5% manganese represent a critical transition composition where the oxidation morphology shifts from large iron-rich oxide nodules to smaller manganese-rich nodules, thereby influencing the protective scale formation and oxidation resistance of Fe-Mn-Al alloys.
